text.title: AI-Assisted Persona Building Method
text.summary: Data-driven persona building process with AI clustering, JTBD integration, and limitations of behavioral-data-only approaches.

rule.statement: Build personas from actual user data (analytics, interviews, surveys), not from assumptions; without data the result is a synthetic persona, not a data-driven one.
rule.rationale: Assumption-based personas tell engineers what they already assume. Data-derived segments surface behavioral patterns that intuition misses and that are defensible to stakeholders.

list.item: 1. Aggregate user data (analytics, CRM, interview transcripts, survey responses)
2. AI clustering of behavioral patterns to identify segment boundaries
3. AI-generated persona drafts from validated cluster centroids
4. Human validation and refinement — researcher names and labels each cluster
5. Ongoing persona updates from new data on a defined refresh cadence
Demographics + digital behavior (not just age and location)
Goals framed as JTBD: "When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]"
Pain points with quantified impact where data supports it
Behavior with trigger analysis: what event causes them to seek this product
Representative quotes backed by sentiment patterns from interview data

rule.statement: Use descriptive behavioral labels for clusters ("Power users who export weekly"), not demographic labels ("35-45 year old managers").
rule.rationale: Demographic labels introduce bias and tell designers what they already assumed. Behavioral labels are actionable for feature design and copy.

rule.statement: Include a persona retirement date in the document; personas without expiry become organizational mythology within 6-12 months.
rule.rationale: User behavior drifts with product changes, market shifts, and cohort turnover. Undated personas cannot be audited for staleness and mislead roadmap decisions.

rule.statement: Always combine behavioral data with qualitative data when generating JTBD statements; behavioral-data-only JTBD produces feature descriptions, not motivations.
rule.rationale: Analytics capture what users do, not why. JTBD inference from behavioral data alone produces statements like "wants to export data" when the underlying motivation is "needs to share progress with their manager to justify the tool spend."

list.item: K-means requires a predefined k; agents choosing k arbitrarily produce misleading persona counts
Behavioral data underrepresents users active offline or on untracked channels
AI-generated persona narratives tend toward archetypes, not individuals
Personas built from digital behavior alone cannot capture motivation or context

text.reference: K-means clustering on behavioral CSV with cluster summary output
Silhouette scoring to find optimal k and flag weak clusters
